content: Metabolic syndrome management targets individual components and overall CV risk. Lifestyle modification is cornerstone: weight loss (5-10% reduces all components), Mediterranean or DASH diet, physical activity (≥150 min/week moderate aerobic plus resistance training), smoking cessation, sleep optimization, stress reduction. Pharmacologic treatment: statins for dyslipidemia (primary therapy regardless of metabolic syndrome diagnosis), antihypertensives (ACEi/ARB preferred—may improve insulin sensitivity), metformin for prediabetes/diabetes (Diabetes Prevention Program showed 31% diabetes risk reduction with lifestyle vs 58% with metformin), GLP-1 receptor agonists for obesity/diabetes (promote weight loss, CV benefit), SGLT2 inhibitors for diabetes/CVD/HF (CV and renal benefits). Antiplatelet therapy (aspirin) considered based on overall ASCVD risk. Treat NAFLD with weight loss, vitamin E in select non-diabetic patients. Address sleep apnea with CPAP. No single medication treats all components—comprehensive risk factor management essential.
